opportunity_map.title: Generate Imp test suites for the 19 untested Dijkstra STS rules using Conway Imp patterns as templates
opportunity_map.evidence: 23 Dijkstra Rules modules in eras/dijkstra/impl/src/Cardano/Ledger/Dijkstra/Rules/. Only 4 Imp test files in testlib Imp/ (UtxowSpec, CertSpec, UtxoSpec, LedgerSpec). Conway has 13 Imp specs as templates. Dijkstra Rules is highest-churn area (19 changes in last 100 commits).

seen_in.outcome: Conway has 13 Imp specs. Dijkstra coverage in early stages.

opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: All citations verified. Specific to this repo. Conway templates exist. Dijkstra is highest-churn area.

opportunity_map.title: Create a substantive CLAUDE.md covering ledger architecture, era boundaries, STS framework, security-critical paths, and build commands
opportunity_map.evidence: CLAUDE.md absent. 2 AI-attributed commits (b566f359, d20b44d3). PR #5670 adds Claude Code skill (.claude/skills/update-changelogs/). 28 cabal packages, 9 eras, nix builds.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: CLAUDE.md absence verified. AI adoption growing. Repo complexity makes this genuinely high-value.

opportunity_map.title: Create .aiignore to protect consensus rules, cryptographic paths, and era transition logic from unreviewed AI modifications
opportunity_map.evidence: .aiignore absent. TPraos rules at libs/cardano-protocol-tpraos/src/Cardano/Protocol/TPraos/Rules/ (OCert, Overlay, Prtcl, Tickn, Updn). STS rules per era. Core ledger paths. AI commits touch CDDL/test files across eras.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Paths verified. Security-critical nature confirmed. AI already active in adjacent areas.

opportunity_map.title: Generate Haddock documentation for undocumented exported modules in Conway and Dijkstra eras
opportunity_map.evidence: Sampled Conway files: PParams.hs (7/1343 lines), Genesis.hs (0/111), BlockBody.hs (0/16), Translation.hs (0/190), TxOut.hs (0/81). Sub-1% density. gh-pages.yml deploys Haddock.

seen_in.outcome: Sub-1% Haddock coverage in sampled era modules

opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Haddock density verified (worse than claimed). Specific modules named. Haddock infra deployed.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to identify divergence points between Agda formal spec and Dijkstra Haskell implementation for conformance test expansion
opportunity_map.evidence: formal-ledger-specifications pinned in cabal.project (tag 6158038b). libs/cardano-ledger-conformance/ with Conway conformance (Imp/Conway/, Spec/Conway/, ExecSpecRule/ with 14+ modules). No Dijkstra conformance directory.

seen_in.outcome: Complete Agda formal spec for Conway, partial for earlier eras.

opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Conformance gap for Dijkstra verified. Conway conformance exists as template.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to write constrained generators for new Dijkstra data types, using existing Conway generators as reference
opportunity_map.evidence: constrained-generators pinned in cabal.project. AI commits for plutusScriptGen confirmed. Dijkstra Sub* modules verified (10 Sub* rule files). Conway testlib exists as reference.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: AI precedent for generator work verified. Sub* modules are Dijkstra-specific.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to generate Dijkstra era transition documentation from Conway-to-Dijkstra diff and NewEra.md guide
opportunity_map.evidence: docs/NewEra.md confirmed. eras/dijkstra/impl/src/Cardano/Ledger/Dijkstra/Translation.hs confirmed. Dijkstra is highest-churn era.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: NewEra.md verified. Dijkstra Translation.hs verified. Specific audit task is concrete.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to identify untested CDDL schema variants in Dijkstra era CddlSpec, particularly for new Sub* rule serialization
opportunity_map.evidence: 8 CDDL files (one per era including dijkstra.cddl). AI commits fixed CDDL test generation. 6 xdescribe'd test groups in Dijkstra CddlSpec.

seen_in.outcome: CddlSpec.hs covers main types, some variants untested

opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: CDDL infra verified. AI already worked in this area. Sub* modules are Dijkstra-specific.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to discover untested corner cases in Dijkstra era constrained generators
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Stage A: Near-duplicate of constrained-gen opportunity. No specific invariant gaps named. Generic enough to apply to any property-testing codebase.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI for state transition debugging in Dijkstra STS rules
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Stage A: No specific bugs, debugging scenarios, or known spec divergence named. A team would ask 'debug what?'

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to review PRs that modify STS rules across multiple eras
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Stage A: No analysis of what AI review would catch that 5+ human reviewers and CI do not. PR template already has CDDL checklist.

adoption_state.evidence: No AI-attributed commits in Imp test files. No AI config referencing test generation. No observable AI attribution found — this does not confirm absence of AI use.
CLAUDE.md does not exist. The opportunity is to create it.
.aiignore does not exist. The opportunity is to create it.
No AI-attributed commits in documentation files. No observable AI attribution found — this does not confirm absence of AI use.
No AI-attributed commits in conformance test files. No observable AI attribution found.
2 Claude co-authored commits (b566f359, d20b44d3, 2026-03-16) added plutusScriptGen for CDDL test generation — related generator authoring but not systematic constrained-generator usage.
No AI-attributed commits in docs/ or Translation.hs modules. No observable AI attribution found.
2 Claude co-authored commits (b566f359, d20b44d3) specifically fixed CDDL test generation issues — AI has been used in this exact area but not systematically.

risk_surface.evidence: Consensus rules (OCert, Overlay, Prtcl, Tickn, Updn). No AI-attributed commits in this path. Heavy testing via property tests.
STS rules per era — financial state transitions, consensus-critical. Property tests + Imp tests cover main paths but edge cases possible. No AI-attributed commits in rule implementation files.
Core ledger state and types. High fan-in (imported by all era packages). No AI-attributed commits.

CDDL specs and test generators. AI exposure confirmed: commits b566f359 and d20b44d3 modified HuddleSpec.hs across alonzo, babbage, conway, shelley, core, and dijkstra. CddlSpec.hs also modified. Tests verify serialization conformance — errors caught by CI.
Conformance bridge between Agda spec and Haskell implementation. Conway conformance complete. No AI-attributed commits.

recommendations.title: Generate Imp test suites for 19 untested Dijkstra STS rules, starting with SubGov and SubUtxo
recommendations.measurable_outcome: eras/dijkstra/impl/testlib/Test/Cardano/Ledger/Dijkstra/Imp/ contains ≥8 *Spec.hs files (doubling current 4) covering SubGovSpec, SubUtxoSpec, GovSpec, GovCertSpec, CertsSpec, BbodySpec, UtxosSpec, and EpochSpec
recommendations.recommended_learning: Pick SubGovSpec as first target. Give Claude the Conway GovSpec.hs from eras/conway/impl/testlib/Test/Cardano/Ledger/Conway/Imp/GovSpec.hs as template + Dijkstra SubGov.hs rule module. Ask: 'Generate an Imp test suite following Conway patterns.' Verify generated tests exercise preconditions and postconditions.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: All citations verified. Measurable outcome binary and verifiable. Conway template path confirmed.

recommendations.title: Write CLAUDE.md covering 28-package architecture, era boundaries, STS framework, nix build commands, and security-critical paths
recommendations.measurable_outcome: CLAUDE.md exists at repo root with ≥200 words covering: (1) multi-era architecture + package map, (2) STS framework overview, (3) cabal + nix build commands, (4) security-critical paths, (5) testing strategy, (6) coding conventions
recommendations.recommended_learning: Use .claude/skills/update-changelogs/SKILL.md as reference for Claude Code integration style. Start with one paragraph per category. Reference docs/NewEra.md for architecture, CONTRIBUTING.md for workflow, cabal.project packages list for module boundaries.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: CLAUDE.md absence verified. Measurable outcome binary. Low effort, high leverage.

recommendations.title: Create .aiignore listing consensus rules (TPraos), core STS rules per era, and serialization-critical CDDL definitions
recommendations.measurable_outcome: .aiignore exists at repo root listing ≥5 paths including libs/cardano-protocol-tpraos/src/Cardano/Protocol/TPraos/Rules/, eras/*/impl/src/Cardano/Ledger/*/Rules/, libs/cardano-ledger-core/src/Cardano/Ledger/Core/, eras/*/impl/cddl/data/*.cddl, and libs/small-steps/
recommendations.recommended_learning: Review with the ledger security lead. Start with consensus paths (TPraos) and STS rule implementations. Use .gitignore syntax. Note: .aiignore should mark paths as review-required, not blocked — AI can still assist with understanding, just not unreviewed modifications.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: All paths verified. Measurable outcome binary.

recommendations.title: Extend conformance testing to Dijkstra era by using AI to identify spec-implementation divergence in Sub* rules
recommendations.measurable_outcome: libs/cardano-ledger-conformance/test/Test/Cardano/Ledger/Conformance/ contains a Dijkstra/ subdirectory with ≥1 conformance test module bridging Agda-extracted spec to Dijkstra implementation
recommendations.recommended_learning: Study libs/cardano-ledger-conformance/test/Test/Cardano/Ledger/Conformance/Imp/Conway/ as reference. Give Claude the Conway conformance module + Dijkstra rule module + relevant Agda spec extract. Ask: 'Where does Dijkstra diverge from the formal spec?'
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: Conway conformance infrastructure confirmed (14+ modules). No Dijkstra conformance exists. Measurable outcome binary.

recommendations.title: Use AI to audit Dijkstra era completeness against NewEra.md checklist and generate missing transition documentation
recommendations.measurable_outcome: docs/ contains a Dijkstra-specific transition status document or docs/NewEra.md is updated with a Dijkstra completion checklist showing which steps are done and which remain
recommendations.recommended_learning: Give Claude docs/NewEra.md + eras/dijkstra/impl/ file listing + eras/conway/impl/ file listing. Ask: 'Comparing Dijkstra to the NewEra.md checklist and Conway, what is missing in Dijkstra?' Review against team roadmap.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: NewEra.md confirmed. Dijkstra Translation.hs confirmed. Measurable outcome verifiable.

recommendations.title: Generate Haddock docs for Conway and Dijkstra exported modules, starting with high-fan-in modules in cardano-ledger-core
recommendations.measurable_outcome: 10 specified modules with currently 0 Haddock comments (Conway/Translation.hs, Conway/TxOut.hs, Conway/BlockBody.hs, Conway/Genesis.hs, and 6 equivalent Dijkstra modules) each have ≥5 Haddock doc comments on exported functions
recommendations.recommended_learning: Pick libs/cardano-ledger-core/src/Cardano/Ledger/Core.hs (highest fan-in). Give Claude the module + 2-3 importing modules for usage context. Ask: 'Draft Haddock comments for all exported functions.' Review for domain accuracy.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: Original measurable outcome too weak — some modules already pass. Revised to target modules with 0 current comments. Re-submitting with fixed outcome for record.

recommendations.title: Systematize AI-assisted constrained generator authoring for Dijkstra Sub* data types
recommendations.measurable_outcome: eras/dijkstra/impl/testlib/ contains ≥3 new generator modules covering Sub* data types
recommendations.recommended_learning: Follow the proven plutusScriptGen pattern from commits b566f359/d20b44d3.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: Dijkstra Arbitrary.hs already contains instances for all 10 Sub* types. Measurable outcome factually incorrect. Type mismatch: start_now invalid for Adoption=Partial.

recommendations.title: Expand Dijkstra CDDL test coverage to Sub* rule serialization variants
recommendations.recommended_learning: Give Claude dijkstra.cddl + CddlSpec.hs + HuddleSpec.hs. Ask: 'Which CDDL alternatives are not covered?'
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Stage B: Real gap is 6 xdescribe'd test groups, not missing schema branches. Measurable outcome ambiguous. Type mismatch: start_now invalid for Adoption=Partial.

quadrant.position: High potential, low activity — prime candidate for guided AI adoption
